_comment: Humanized via the humanizer skill. Warm if/then phrasing, concrete verbs, spoken rhythm, contractions, numbers spelled out. Cloned from reference.wav via fp16.

sections.text: If you run a bakery, every morning starts with the same guess. How much should I bake today? Because if you bake too much, the leftovers just get thrown out. And if you don't bake enough, you run out, and you're turning customers away.

This is where BakerySense comes in. You open the app, and it tells you exactly how much to bake. So today, it might say ninety croissants, instead of the hundred and twenty you made yesterday. And it shows you how much you'd waste, and how likely you are to sell out.
And getting started is easy. You just upload your past sales, or connect your till. Give it a couple of weeks of history, and it starts to learn how your shop runs.
But here's the part that makes it really special. Every night, all on its own, it looks back at what it guessed, and checks it against what you actually sold.

So let's say it kept telling you to bake too many croissants on Wednesdays. It notices that pattern. Then it suggests a small change, and tests it against your past sales first, just to make sure it really helps.
All you do is tap approve. And just like that, tomorrow's plan gets a little better. You're always the one making the call.
And under the hood, every fix is a versioned skill. Diagnosed, tested, and approved. So you can always see what changed, and roll it back any time.
And every shop is a little different, so each one picks up its own habits. Maybe one learns to ease off on Wednesdays, while another finds it's really Sundays that are slow.
And that's BakerySense. It doesn't just guess what to bake. It genuinely learns your bakery, and gets a little better every day.
